text.title: SSH Port Change Procedure
text.summary: Safe sequence for changing the SSH port on Ubuntu 24.04: open new UFW port first, then update systemd socket override, restart, verify in a new terminal — never close the original session until confirmed working.

rule.statement: On Ubuntu 24.04, SSH uses systemd socket activation. Changing Port in sshd_config alone does NOT change the listening port. The systemd socket override file must be updated: /etc/systemd/system/ssh.socket.d/override.conf
rule.rationale: The ssh.socket unit controls which port the daemon listens on via socket activation. The Port directive in sshd_config is ignored when socket activation is in use.

example.note: ListenStream= (empty) clears the default port 22. ListenStream=2222 sets the new port.

rule.statement: Execute port change in this exact order: (1) open new port in UFW, (2) create socket override, (3) reload daemon and restart socket, (4) test connection on new port in a NEW terminal, (5) only then remove old port from UFW.
rule.rationale: If the new port is not opened in UFW before restarting SSH, you will be locked out. If the connection test fails and you closed the original session, you need console access to recover.

example.code:
# Step 1: Open new port FIRST sudo ufw allow 2222/tcp comment "SSH"
# Step 2: Create socket override sudo mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/ssh.socket.d sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/ssh.socket.d/override.conf <<'EOF' [Socket] ListenStream= ListenStream=2222 EOF
# Step 3: Reload and restart sudo systemctl daemon-reload sudo systemctl restart ssh.socket sudo systemctl restart ssh
# Step 4: Test in a NEW terminal before closing current session # ssh -p 2222 nero@server
# Step 5: After confirming — remove old port sudo ufw delete allow 22/tcp

list.item: Update fail2ban SSH jail port to match new port
Remove weak host keys: sudo rm -f /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key* /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key*
Update monitoring alerts if they check SSH port availability
